page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u0.c1m2.xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 processor. It features 4 physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz, supported by a 33 MB L3 cache. The instance is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It operates on the KVM hypervisor, lacks local storage, and includes a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. No GPU accelerators are present on this hardware configuration.

Benchmark results place this instance in the lower performance tiers. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, consistently rank in the bottom 25% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, with uncached read speeds of 19.91 GB/sec falling into the bottom 10% tier. Similarly, static web serving throughput of 2.54 GB/sec and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression are rated as poor. Cryptographic and integer operations are supported via hardware flags like AES and BMI2, though overall execution speeds remain modest.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.u0.c1m2.xlarge represents a budget-friendly utility tier. Its dedicated CPU allocation avoids the unpredictability of shared-core burstable instances, but its resource density and low memory bandwidth limit its suitability for demanding applications. It is best suited for lightweight, non-critical workloads such as basic web serving, small-scale development environments, simple task queues, or low-throughput utility services that do not require high memory capacity or rapid disk I/O.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u0.c1m2.xlarge is an entry-level x86_64 server featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz and 8.0 GB of system memory. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or GPU acceleration, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates modest performance, with CPU and memory bandwidth metrics consistently placing in the bottom 10% to 25% of tested servers. Uncached memory read speeds reach 19.91 GB/sec, and static web serving throughput is limited to 2.54 GB/sec. Given its low resource density and weak performance profile, this instance is positioned as a highly cost-efficient option for lightweight workloads. It is best suited for basic utility tasks, small development environments, and low-traffic web serving where dedicated CPU allocation is preferred over shared-core burstable alternatives.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u0.c1m2.xlarge is a KVM-based x86_64 server with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline, offering budget-friendly hosting for lightweight utility workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u0.c1m2.xlarge is a budget x86_64 server with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 8.0 GB RAM, designed for lightweight utility workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly, dedicated-core server configured with modest memory and processing power for basic utility and development workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs at 2.5 GHz
8.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core)
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Best suited for lightweight utility tasks and development environments
